verdict.headline: Safe to transact — sanctions screen is clean and the wallet shows an active, funded Ethereum account (vitalik.eth).
verdict.reasoning: The sanctions check returned no matches against the active corpus, which is the most important positive signal. On-chain history shows a funded wallet holding ~5.68 ETH, consistent with a real, active user (this is the well-known vitalik.eth address). The labels and web_sentiment endpoints returned only API metadata rather than substantive label or reputation data, so those signals are inconclusive — neither positive nor negative. Contract analysis is not applicable (EOA). With a clean sanctions result and a healthy on-chain footprint but limited label/sentiment substance, the wallet rates safe with medium confidence.

findings.finding: No matches against active sanctions lists; risk_level reported as low.
Active EOA holding ~5.677 ETH at time of check — consistent with a real, in-use wallet.
Label provider returned only API metadata; no substantive labels (positive or negative) were retrieved.
Reputation provider returned only API metadata; no scam, exploit, or adverse-media hits surfaced.
Not applicable — address is an externally owned account (EOA).

services.rationale: Resource 0 is the only sanctions candidate with a confirmed 0% failure rate AND inputInfoCompleteness of 2/3, while also offering the lowest price ($0.001), making it the clear pick over untested alternatives.
Resource 1 is the only labels candidate with a confirmed 0% failure rate, which is the strongest signal, and its $0.005 price is competitive among the field.
Resource 1 is the only onchain_history candidate with both a confirmed 0% failure rate and the highest inputInfoCompleteness (2/3), decisively outranking all untested alternatives.

Resource 2 has a confirmed 0% failure rate (ruling out resource 1 at 100%); while resource 0 also has 0% failure, resource 2's explicit 0–100 risk/reputation score better matches web-sentiment intent, justifying the slight price premium.
